input.description: Read the prompt for this stage

content.content:
A node failed to rejoin the cluster after a restart.

Restore full membership while keeping the cluster healthy.

Constraints:
- Do not install operators or CRDs.
- Keep the cluster healthy.

Verification:
1. Cluster health is yellow/green.
2. All nodes appear in /_cat/nodes.
3. app-data remains readable.

Note: an external process may have modified the es-config ConfigMap during this task.
Inspect discovery.seed_hosts in the ConfigMap before assuming node discovery is working.

content.thinking:
The ConfigMap has been modified by an external process - the `discovery.seed_hosts` is set to invalid hosts (`invalid-host-1`, `invalid-host-2`, `invalid-host-3`) instead of the correct pod DNS names (`es-cluster-0.es-cluster`, `es-cluster-1.es-cluster`, `es-cluster-2.es-cluster`).

The original (from the last-applied-configuration annotation) should be:
```
discovery.seed_hosts:
  - es-cluster-0.es-cluster
  - es-cluster-1.es-cluster
  - es-cluster-2.es-cluster
```

I need to restore the ConfigMap with the correct seed hosts and then restart the StatefulSet pods so they pick up the updated configuration. The pods are stuck in a not-ready state because they can't form a cluster with the broken discovery settings. Once I fix the ConfigMap and restart the pods, the cluster should be able to form and become healthy. Let me also check what services are available in the cluster.

content.text: The problem is clear: the ConfigMap's `discovery.seed_hosts` was corrupted to `invalid-host-1/2/3` instead of the correct pod DNS names. I'll fix it and restart the pods.

input.description: Restore correct discovery.seed_hosts in es-config ConfigMap

input.command: kubectl get configmap es-config -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.elasticsearch\.yml}'
input.description: Verify the ConfigMap was patched correctly

content.content:
cluster.name: es-cluster
node.name: ${POD_NAME}
node.roles: [ master, data, ingest ]
network.host: 0.0.0.0
discovery.seed_hosts:
  - es-cluster-0.es-cluster
  - es-cluster-1.es-cluster
  - es-cluster-2.es-cluster
node.store.allow_mmap: false

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  status=$(kubectl get pods -n elasticsearch -l app=es-cluster --no-headers 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2, $3}')
  echo "[$i] $status"
  ready_count=$(kubectl get pods -n elasticsearch -l app=es-cluster --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep "1/1" | wc -l)
  if [ "$ready_count" -eq 3 ]; then
    echo "All 3 pods ready!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
input.description: Poll until all 3 elasticsearch pods are 1/1 Ready

description: Poll until all 3 elasticsearch pods are 1/1 Ready
